classification_evidence: The requested change affects ADLC workflow primitives, skills, schemas, and scripts rather than a product runtime path.
Compound engineering performance depends on deterministic reuse of prior implementation learnings and bounded resume state.

supported.claim: ADLC already has graph-backed research, Build Brief decomposition, readiness-gated work item emission, and bounded verifier-first execution.
supported.extracted_from: ADLC repo review and Graphify query output

supported.claim: The useful CE concepts for ADLC are a concrete learning store, learning capture, learning refresh, task identity, resume fingerprints, worktree isolation, and script-first preprocessing.
The whole CE plugin should not be imported because ADLC already owns decomposition, review, validation, and graph comprehension surfaces.

supported_claims: Implement only bounded ADLC primitives that improve compound execution performance.
Keep optional session-history search out of the required planning path.
suspect_phrases: auto-run learning refresh on every task
make Proof or CE sessions a core ADLC dependency

section_policy.reason: The decomposition depends on repo-grounded CE research and ADLC primitive mapping.
The user asked ADLC to decompose implementation work into shippable tasks.
The implementation touches schema and emitter behavior, so executable validation is required.
The proposed capability adds narrowly scoped ADLC skills for capture and refresh.

primary_verifier.rationale: The decomposition is only useful if ADLC can emit ready, normalized implementation work items.
primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: missing or blocked compound engineering decomposition

secondary_verifiers.rationale: The decomposition must satisfy the ADLC Build Brief schema.

verification_spec.scope_note: Validate the plan artifact before any implementation task starts.

enterprise_readiness_contract.production_grade_target: Add compound-engineering capability to ADLC through small, reusable primitives: durable learnings, deterministic refresh, stable task identity, resumable execution fingerprints, isolated worktrees, and script-first context preprocessing.
enterprise_readiness_contract.backward_compatibility: Existing Build Briefs, ADLC CLI commands, Graphify research workflow, and work-item emitter behavior must continue to work without the new fields or skills.
enterprise_readiness_contract.forward_compatibility: New learning and fingerprint metadata must be optional and structured so future providers can consume it without changing the core Build Brief contract again.
failure_mode_coverage: Stale or duplicate learnings are covered by explicit refresh decisions and consolidation rules.
Task identity drift is covered by stable IDs and fingerprint checks tied to verifier state.
Worktree collisions are covered by an isolation contract that records branch, base, dirty-state, and cleanup behavior.
Context bloat is covered by script-first preprocessing that passes paths and summaries instead of raw file dumps.
definition_of_done: ADLC can capture implementation learnings into a repo-local docs/solutions store without manual format drift.
ADLC can refresh learnings with Keep, Update, Consolidate, Replace, and Delete outcomes.
Work items emitted from Build Briefs carry stable identity and optional resume fingerprint metadata.
Compound implementation work documents worktree isolation and resume requirements before coding starts.
All changed contracts are covered by schema validation, CLI tests, and contract tests.
enterprise_readiness_contract.compliance_posture: No regulated user data, secrets, auth, or external service mutation is introduced. The persistent store is repo-local documentation plus optional metadata in existing ADLC artifacts.
production_invariant_coverage: idempotency: reuse existing ADLC idempotency keys for emitted work items and add optional stable task identity fields.
persistence: store learnings as versioned repo documentation rather than opaque agent memory.
observability: refresh decisions and resume fingerprints must be visible in Build Brief evidence and workflow state.
sensitive_data: learning capture must reject secrets and environment-specific credentials.

sections.1_context: ADLC should support compound engineering as a first-class execution pattern, but the EveryInc CE plugin should be treated as research input rather than a dependency. The goal is faster repeated implementation through durable learnings, stable task identity, and bounded resume behavior while preserving ADLC's existing Graphify, Build Brief, Eval Council, and emitter primitives.
sections.2_scope_and_constraints: In scope: docs/solutions learning store, learning capture skill, learning refresh skill, optional task identity and resume fingerprint metadata, worktree isolation contract, and script-first preflight. Out of scope: importing the CE plugin, adding a marketplace installer, making Proof mandatory, adding a parallel CE planner/reviewer stack, or requiring session-history search for normal ADLC planning.
sections.3_repo_findings: ADLC already has repo graph research, Build Brief schemas, reuse analysis, feedback loop, codegen context, workflow state, and readiness-gated work-item emission. The missing pieces are concrete durable learnings, deterministic learning refresh, task-level resume identity, and a compound worktree/session contract.
sections.4_architecture: Add the capability as small ADLC-native extensions. The learning store is markdown under docs/solutions plus templates and validation guidance. Skills perform capture and refresh around that store. The Build Brief schema and emitter preserve optional stable identity and resume metadata. Worktree isolation is a documented execution contract. Script-first preflight creates compact context artifacts for agents and Graphify instead of passing large raw content.

5_security_review.risk: Learning capture can persist secrets or environment-specific credentials.
5_security_review.mitigation: Require secret scanning guidance, explicit redaction, and no captured tokens, keys, private URLs, or user-specific paths unless they are already documented repo fixtures.

5_security_review.risk: Refresh automation can delete useful knowledge.
5_security_review.mitigation: Require explicit Keep, Update, Consolidate, Replace, or Delete decisions with evidence links; do not auto-delete without recorded rationale.

sections.6_observability_slo: Every capture and refresh run should leave a reviewable artifact path, outcome, source evidence, and verifier command. Resume fingerprints should expose task id, verifier status, branch/base, and relevant input hashes.
sections.7_execution_plan: Ship this in six bounded implementation tasks plus one validation task. Start with a learning-store spec, then add capture and refresh skills, then add optional stable identity and resume metadata, then harden worktree/session execution, then add script-first preflight. Validate with ADLC schema checks, emitter dry-runs, CLI tests, and Graphify review.

8_task_tickets.title: Define ADLC learning store
8_task_tickets.objective: Create the repo-local learning store contract that turns repeated implementation discoveries into reusable ADLC knowledge.

decision_contract.resolution: The research already supports a bounded docs/solutions store; no Type 1 decision is required.

scope: Create docs/specs/compound-engineering-learning-store.md.
Create docs/solutions/README.md and a reusable solution template.
Define required fields for problem, context, decision, implementation pattern, verifier evidence, stale conditions, and consolidation links.
Document what must not be captured, including secrets, raw private paths, and unsupported claims.
out_of_scope: No database, vector store, or external memory service.
No automatic background indexing.
No import of EveryInc CE plugin files.

acceptance_criteria.given: a future ADLC implementation finds a reusable technique
acceptance_criteria.when: the contributor consults the learning-store spec
acceptance_criteria.then: the contributor can create a solution note with required evidence, verifier, and stale-condition fields
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: docs/specs/compound-engineering-learning-store.md and docs/solutions/_template.md exist and name all required fields

acceptance_criteria.given: the CE plugin contains broader session and installer features
acceptance_criteria.then: it explicitly excludes wholesale plugin import and external persistent services
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: the spec has an out-of-scope section covering plugin import, marketplace install, and external stores

anti_slop_rules: Do not create generic knowledge-base prose without required fields.
Do not make session-history search a prerequisite for using ADLC.
Do not duplicate existing reuse-analysis or feedback-loop skill responsibilities.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: ADLC has skills and feedback loops but no concrete durable learning artifact format.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: Automated indexing and richer search ranking can wait until a real learning corpus exists.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: A markdown store with explicit required fields is reviewable, versioned, and enough for first-slice reuse.

compatibility_contract.backward: Existing ADLC workflows continue without docs/solutions entries.
compatibility_contract.forward: The template must allow later CLI validation or Graphify indexing without changing captured content semantics.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: No migration; introduce the store with documentation and an empty template.

implementation_notes: Use the research artifact as the source of truth for what CE concepts are worth adopting.
Prefer a small schema-like markdown template over a new JSON schema in this slice.
Cross-link existing skills/reuse-analysis and skills/feedback-loop instead of replacing them.

primary_verifier.rationale: The first slice is concrete learning-store artifacts.

secondary_verifiers.rationale: The store must include safety and refresh fields.
secondary_verifiers.expected_failure_mode: learning store safety or refresh fields missing

verification_spec.scope_note: This task is documentation-only and should not add executable dependencies.

evidence_responsibilities: Link the EveryInc CE research artifact.
Show the learning-store files and their required fields.
Show the out-of-scope guardrails.
definition_of_done: Learning-store spec and template exist.
The spec says when to capture, when not to capture, and how to mark stale learnings.
The spec references existing ADLC reuse and feedback primitives instead of duplicating them.
failure_modes: MEDIUM: A vague learning store becomes unreviewable notes; mitigation: required fields and verifier evidence.
MEDIUM: The store becomes a second planning system; mitigation: keep it as evidence consumed by Build Briefs and skills.

evidence: Learning entries live in versioned repo docs.

8_task_tickets.title: Add learning capture skill
8_task_tickets.objective: Add a narrow ADLC skill that captures proven implementation lessons into the learning store after validation.

decision_contract.resolution: Capture is approved as a manual, verifier-gated skill rather than an automatic memory writer.

scope: Create skills/learning-capture/SKILL.md.
Update skills/manifest.json if the manifest lists repository skills explicitly.
Require source evidence, verifier evidence, reuse conditions, and stale conditions before writing a solution note.
Require redaction review before persisting any command output or path-sensitive details.
out_of_scope: No automatic capture on every agent run.
No external memory API.
No unreviewed edits to existing solution notes.

acceptance_criteria.given: a validated implementation produces a reusable pattern
acceptance_criteria.then: the skill guides the agent to write a solution note using the approved template
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: skills/learning-capture/SKILL.md names required evidence, verifier, stale-condition, and redaction steps

acceptance_criteria.given: the reusable pattern is not yet validated
acceptance_criteria.then: the skill refuses durable capture and asks for verifier evidence first
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: the skill contains a validation-before-capture rule

anti_slop_rules: Do not let the skill summarize without citing source files or verifier commands.
Do not store secrets, token values, or private environment paths.
Do not create overlapping skills for reuse analysis.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: Learning capture currently depends on ad hoc post-run summaries.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: CLI validation for solution notes can be added after the markdown contract proves useful.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: A skill-level gate still prevents unsupported durable knowledge from entering the repo.

compatibility_contract.backward: Existing skills and workflows continue unchanged if learning capture is never invoked.
compatibility_contract.forward: Captured notes must keep stable headings so later refresh and indexing tools can parse them.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: No migration; add the skill and start with future notes only.

implementation_notes: Model the skill structure after existing ADLC skills.
Keep the trigger explicit; do not auto-invoke from every code review.
Make source evidence and verifier evidence mandatory.

primary_verifier.rationale: The capture skill must exist and include the required guardrails.
primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: learning capture skill missing or underspecified

secondary_verifiers.rationale: If this repo manifests skills explicitly, the new skill must be discoverable.

verification_spec.scope_note: The skill adds process capability; it should not mutate unrelated ADLC skills.

evidence_responsibilities: Show the skill instructions and trigger conditions.
Show that capture requires verifier evidence.
Show that redaction and unsupported-claim rules are present.
definition_of_done: The learning-capture skill exists.
The skill writes only template-shaped solution notes.
The skill refuses capture without source and verifier evidence.
failure_modes: HIGH: Captured false knowledge slows later compound work; mitigation: validation-before-capture.
HIGH: Captured secrets leak into repo docs; mitigation: redaction checklist and explicit forbidden content.

evidence: Capture skill requires redaction before persistent writes.

8_task_tickets.title: Add learning refresh workflow
8_task_tickets.objective: Add a deterministic refresh workflow that keeps durable ADLC learnings current without broad automatic rewrites.

decision_contract.resolution: Refresh is approved as an explicit skill or script-assisted workflow with recorded outcomes.

scope: Create skills/learning-refresh/SKILL.md.
Define Keep, Update, Consolidate, Replace, and Delete outcomes.
Require evidence for every refresh decision.
Add a lightweight deterministic scanner only if needed to list candidate solution notes.
out_of_scope: No automatic deletion of solution notes.
No agent-written refresh without a diff and rationale.
No broad refresh of every repo doc.

acceptance_criteria.given: a solution note may be stale or duplicated
acceptance_criteria.then: the workflow records one of Keep, Update, Consolidate, Replace, or Delete with evidence
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: skills/learning-refresh/SKILL.md names all five outcomes and their required evidence

acceptance_criteria.given: a refresh would delete or replace a learning
acceptance_criteria.then: it requires explicit rationale and source links
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: the skill has delete and replace safeguards

anti_slop_rules: Do not refresh unrelated documentation.
Do not delete learnings based only on age.
Do not hide consolidation decisions in commit messages only.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: ADLC has feedback loops but not a durable refresh protocol for implementation learnings.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: A richer refresh report format can wait until there are multiple solution notes.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: The five-outcome workflow prevents silent drift and silent deletion.

compatibility_contract.backward: Existing docs remain valid until they opt into solution-note format.
compatibility_contract.forward: Outcome labels must remain stable for future scripts and Graphify indexing.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Introduce refresh workflow without modifying existing docs outside docs/solutions.

implementation_notes: Borrow CE's refresh taxonomy, not its whole session stack.
Prefer deterministic candidate listing over large agent prompts.
Use Graphify as enrichment when graph context is already fresh.

primary_verifier.rationale: The refresh workflow must expose the CE outcome taxonomy and evidence requirements.
primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: learning refresh workflow missing or unsafe

secondary_verifiers.rationale: The store and refresh skill must agree on terminology.
secondary_verifiers.expected_failure_mode: learning store and refresh terminology diverge

verification_spec.scope_note: Refresh must be explicit and evidence-backed.

evidence_responsibilities: Show the five outcome definitions.
Show delete and replace safeguards.
Show that refresh is explicit, not automatic.
definition_of_done: The learning-refresh skill exists.
Refresh outcomes are stable and evidence-backed.
The workflow can be followed without adding an external service.
failure_modes: MEDIUM: Refresh causes broad churn; mitigation: docs/solutions scope and outcome evidence.
HIGH: Useful learnings are deleted; mitigation: explicit Delete rationale and source links.

evidence: Refresh decisions require evidence and stable outcome labels.

8_task_tickets.title: Preserve stable task identity and resume fingerprints
8_task_tickets.objective: Extend ADLC task contracts so compound implementation work can resume by stable task identity and verifier state rather than fragile conversation context.

decision_contract.resolution: Stable task identity and resume fingerprints are optional metadata extensions, so they do not break existing Build Briefs.

scope: Add backwards-compatible optional task metadata for stable task identity and resume fingerprints.
Preserve the metadata in emit-work-items normalized artifacts.
Tie resume fingerprints to target files, verifier command, expected failure mode, and dependency status.
Add CLI and contract tests that prove existing briefs still validate.
out_of_scope: No new workflow database.
No replacement for existing idempotency keys.
No requirement that every legacy task provide fingerprints.

acceptance_criteria.given: a Build Brief task includes stable identity metadata
acceptance_criteria.then: the schema accepts the optional metadata while legacy tasks still validate
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: tests cover both populated and absent metadata

acceptance_criteria.when: a task has resume fingerprint metadata
acceptance_criteria.then: the emitted artifact preserves it alongside verifier and dependency data
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: tests/test_adlc_cli.sh or tests/test_adlc_contracts.sh asserts preserved metadata

anti_slop_rules: Do not rename task_id or idempotency_key semantics.
Do not require fingerprints for old Build Briefs.
Do not store raw prompt history as the fingerprint.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: Current ADLC idempotency is external-emission oriented; it does not describe task-level resume conditions.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: Automated fingerprint recomputation can wait; first slice preserves provided metadata and documents the fields.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: Optional metadata plus tests improves resumability without changing existing execution semantics.

compatibility_contract.backward: All existing Build Brief fixtures and xia-adlc-remediation.json continue to validate.
compatibility_contract.forward: Metadata names must be stable enough for future workflow-state persistence and provider adapters.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Add optional fields, update docs, and keep legacy artifacts untouched unless tests need focused fixtures.

implementation_notes: Implement this as optional fields on the existing task object.
Emit the fields if present and omit them if absent.
Use target_files and expected_failure_mode from verification_spec as part of documented fingerprint composition.

primary_verifier.rationale: Schema and emitter contract changes must be covered by existing contract tests.
primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: stable identity metadata not accepted or not preserved

secondary_verifiers.rationale: CLI normalization must preserve optional resume metadata.
secondary_verifiers.expected_failure_mode: CLI emitter drops optional resume metadata

verification_spec.scope_note: Keep schema changes backwards-compatible and emitter-only for this slice.

evidence_responsibilities: Show schema optional fields.
Show emitter preservation output.
Show legacy fixture validation still passes.
definition_of_done: Optional stable identity and resume fingerprint metadata are documented and schema-valid.
emit-work-items preserves the metadata.
Existing fixtures remain valid.
failure_modes: HIGH: Metadata breaks legacy briefs; mitigation: optional fields and regression tests.
MEDIUM: Fingerprints become conversation hashes; mitigation: define verifier and file based components only.

evidence: Stable task identity complements existing idempotency keys rather than replacing them.
Resume fingerprints tie retries to verifier and dependency state.

8_task_tickets.title: Harden compound worktree and session contract
8_task_tickets.objective: Define the ADLC execution contract for isolated compound work so parallel agents and resumptions do not collide.

decision_contract.resolution: Worktree isolation is a process contract first; automation can follow after the contract is validated.

scope: Create or update ADLC workflow docs with worktree isolation requirements.
Specify branch naming, base commit recording, dirty-worktree handling, dependency order, and cleanup.
Require resume context to include task id, branch, base, modified paths, verifier status, and open blockers.
Document when multiple worktrees are allowed and when a serial implementation lane is required.
out_of_scope: No mandatory new git wrapper command in the first slice.
No automatic merge conflict resolver.
No unmanaged parallel writes to the same files.

acceptance_criteria.given: two implementation tasks can run in parallel
acceptance_criteria.then: each task records branch, base, dirty-state, modified paths, verifier state, and cleanup expectations
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: WORKFLOW.md or docs/specs/session-persistence.md documents these required fields

acceptance_criteria.given: tasks modify overlapping files or have verifier dependencies
acceptance_criteria.then: the contract explains when to keep the work serial
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: the docs name overlap and dependency conditions that block parallel worktrees

anti_slop_rules: Do not promise safe parallelism without file-overlap checks.
Do not create a new branch-management abstraction before documenting the contract.
Do not discard dirty user changes.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: ADLC has workflow state but lacks explicit compound worktree isolation rules.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: Automated worktree creation can wait until manual contract usage is proven.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: Documentation plus resume metadata prevents unsafe ad hoc parallelism.

compatibility_contract.forward: The contract should be implementable later as a CLI command without changing task metadata.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Document the contract and add tests only if docs include executable examples.

implementation_notes: Use existing session persistence docs as the natural home for resume context.
Keep rules concrete and enforceable.
Explicitly preserve user changes and dirty-worktree constraints.

primary_verifier.rationale: The worktree/session contract must be visible in ADLC workflow documentation.

secondary_verifiers.rationale: The contract must distinguish parallel-safe and serial work.
secondary_verifiers.expected_failure_mode: parallel versus serial worktree rules missing

verification_spec.scope_note: This is a contract hardening task, not a git automation task.

evidence_responsibilities: Show branch/base/dirty-state requirements.
Show parallel versus serial rules.
Show that dirty user changes are protected.
definition_of_done: ADLC docs describe compound worktree isolation.
Resume context requirements are explicit.
Parallelism guardrails are documented.
failure_modes: HIGH: Parallel tasks overwrite each other; mitigation: file-overlap and dependency rules.
HIGH: User work is lost; mitigation: dirty-worktree checks and no destructive commands.

evidence: Parallel work is allowed only when dependencies and file overlap permit it.

8_task_tickets.title: Add script-first compound context preflight
8_task_tickets.objective: Reduce compound-engineering context cost by generating deterministic compact context artifacts before agent work.

decision_contract.resolution: Script-first preflight is approved as deterministic local preprocessing that feeds ADLC and agents with paths and summaries.

scope: Add a deterministic script or ADLC CLI subcommand that summarizes solution notes, Build Brief task metadata, verifier targets, and graph evidence paths.
Pass paths and compact summaries to agents instead of raw large content.
Document how Graphify output is used as enrichment when present.
Add tests for deterministic output and missing-optional-input behavior.
out_of_scope: No LLM ranking inside the script.
No requirement that graphify-out be committed.
No broad filesystem crawl outside the repo root.

acceptance_criteria.given: a Build Brief and optional solution notes exist
acceptance_criteria.then: it emits deterministic JSON or markdown with task ids, relevant paths, verifier commands, and learning references
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: a test asserts stable output for a fixture

acceptance_criteria.then: it reports absent optional inputs and still returns usable task context
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: a test covers missing optional input behavior

anti_slop_rules: Do not dump full source files into preflight output.
Do not call an LLM from deterministic preprocessing.
Do not fail just because graphify-out is absent.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: ADLC agents can consume large context manually; no compact compound preflight exists.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: Ranking relevance with embeddings or LLMs can wait; deterministic path summaries are enough for first slice.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: Stable preflight output reduces token use and is easy to test.

compatibility_contract.backward: Existing ADLC CLI commands and Build Briefs continue to run if preflight is unused.
compatibility_contract.forward: Output should be machine-readable enough for future agents and provider adapters.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Add as an optional script or subcommand with tests and docs.

implementation_notes: Prefer stdlib-only parsing and existing ADLC helpers.
Use structured JSON for machine consumption and optional markdown for humans.
Treat Graphify report paths as optional enrichment.

primary_verifier.rationale: The preflight script or CLI command must be covered by ADLC CLI tests.
primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: compound context preflight missing or non-deterministic

secondary_verifiers.rationale: Any CLI changes must remain syntactically valid.

verification_spec.scope_note: Preflight must be local, deterministic, and optional.

evidence_responsibilities: Show deterministic fixture output.
Show behavior when optional graph and learning inputs are missing.
Show that the output passes paths and summaries, not raw bulk content.
definition_of_done: Compound context preflight exists as a script or ADLC command.
Preflight output is deterministic and compact.
Tests cover normal and missing-optional-input paths.
failure_modes: MEDIUM: Preflight becomes another large prompt dump; mitigation: path and summary limits.
MEDIUM: Preflight depends on generated graph artifacts; mitigation: optional Graphify enrichment.

evidence: Graphify and learning inputs are optional; missing inputs are reported without blocking task context.
Preflight output names source paths and verifier commands.

8_task_tickets.title: Validate ADLC compound engineering slice
8_task_tickets.objective: Validate the compound-engineering implementation end to end before publishing.

decision_contract.resolution: Validation task only; no Type 1 decision.

scope: Run Build Brief schema validation.
Run ADLC emitter dry-run with --require-ready.
Run CLI and contract tests.
Run Graphify review after implementation and inspect the updated graph report.
out_of_scope: No remote provider mutation unless a maintainer explicitly supplies a provider command.
No PR merge without passing validation or documented blocker.

acceptance_criteria.when: ADLC validates and emits the compound Build Brief
acceptance_criteria.then: the Build Brief is schema-valid and readiness status is ready

acceptance_criteria.given: schema, CLI, and workflow docs changed
acceptance_criteria.then: ADLC CLI and contract tests pass
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: bash tests/test_adlc_cli.sh and bash tests/test_adlc_contracts.sh pass

anti_slop_rules: Do not claim compound engineering support from docs-only changes if schema or emitter behavior is incomplete.
Do not skip Graphify review after code changes.
Do not publish graphify-out unless the repository policy changes.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: The decomposition is intentionally first-slice; richer automation is deferred.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: Provider mutation for GitHub issues and automated worktree creation remain follow-up work.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: The emitted work items are ready and can be executed manually or by a future provider without ambiguity.

compatibility_contract.backward: Existing ADLC tests and fixtures continue to pass.
compatibility_contract.forward: Validation evidence should be sufficient for a follow-up PR to implement GitHub issue mutation if desired.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Ship behind optional docs, skills, and metadata; no default behavior changes without tests.

implementation_notes: Use ADLC itself to validate and emit the task decomposition.
Run Graphify after implementation, not as a substitute for tests.
Stage only intentional repo artifacts; leave generated graphify-out untracked unless maintainers request it.

primary_verifier.rationale: The final plan must be executable as ready GitHub-targeted work items.

secondary_verifiers.rationale: The Build Brief must remain schema-valid.
secondary_verifiers.expected_failure_mode: compound engineering Build Brief schema validation fails

secondary_verifiers.rationale: The implementation must preserve ADLC CLI and schema contracts.
secondary_verifiers.expected_failure_mode: ADLC CLI or contract tests fail

verification_spec.scope_note: Validation is required before remote publish.

evidence_responsibilities: Capture validate-artifact output.
Capture emit-work-items readiness output.
Capture CLI and contract test output.
Capture Graphify review summary after implementation.
definition_of_done: Build Brief validates.
GitHub-targeted work-item dry-run is ready.
ADLC CLI and contract tests pass.
Graphify review is current after implementation.
failure_modes: HIGH: The plan emits blocked work items; mitigation: --require-ready gate.
HIGH: Schema or emitter changes regress existing ADLC usage; mitigation: full CLI and contract tests.

evidence: Validation task requires command evidence before publish.

9_risks: Scope creep: importing CE wholesale would duplicate ADLC planning, review, and validation surfaces.
Knowledge rot: durable learnings can become stale unless refresh outcomes are explicit.
Context bloat: compound preflight must pass compact paths and summaries, not raw content dumps.
Parallel execution risk: worktree isolation must handle dirty state, file overlap, dependencies, and cleanup.
sections.10_rollout: Ship as an ADLC-native first slice. Start with docs and skills, then optional schema and emitter metadata, then workflow docs and script-first preflight. Keep all behavior opt-in until tests prove it.
11_validation_plan: graphify query "What changed in ADLC compound engineering support and what risks remain?" --budget 2500

12_skill_trigger_configuration.trigger: Before implementation and after code changes, refresh or query graph context for ADLC workflow, schema, emitter, and skill relationships.
After a validated implementation produces a reusable solution pattern.
When existing solution notes may be stale, duplicated, or superseded by new implementation evidence.

14_graph_research_evidence.external_graph: graphify update . run in cloned external repo

notes: Graphify research found existing ADLC primitives that reduce the need for new architecture.
Implementation tasks must reuse existing schema, emitter, skill, and workflow files before adding new surfaces.
Generated graphify-out artifacts should remain untracked unless the project decides to version them.
